text.title: AI-Assisted Quarter Retro Synthesis Core Rules
text.summary: Five hard rules for synthesizing a product-dev quarter retro across multiple squads + telemetry sources.

rule.statement: The synthesis MUST ingest all four source types: per-squad retros, ticket export, DORA metrics, incident postmortems. Missing any source produces a partial picture that misses cross-cutting issues. If a source is unavailable, document the gap explicitly.

rationale.source: DORA State of DevOps research; engineering-effectiveness practice

rule.rationale: Squad retros capture team-perceived friction; tickets capture work-shape reality; DORA captures delivery health; incidents capture failure patterns. Any single source misses what the others surface. Quarter retros that ignore DORA stay narrative-rich and decision-poor; retros that ignore incidents repeat last quarter's pain.

rule.statement: A theme in the main retro doc must appear in ≥ 2 squads' input. Single-squad-only issues go to that squad's individual retro, not the cross-squad doc. This prevents one squad's pain from dominating the team-wide narrative.
rule.rationale: Cross-squad retros are for cross-squad patterns. A single squad's tooling complaint is their problem; if 3 squads cite the same tooling, it's a team-wide investment decision. The 2-squad floor keeps the doc focused on org-level themes.

rule.statement: DORA metrics are reported as quarter-over-quarter DELTA, never as standalone snapshots. "Deploy frequency 8/day" means nothing; "9/day → 12/day (+33%)" or "9/day → 6/day (-33% with explanation)" is the story. Trend is the signal.

rationale.source: DORA research methodology; SPC trend analysis

rule.rationale: A snapshot of deploy frequency tells you nothing about whether the team is improving. Quarter-over-quarter delta does. The DORA quartiles (Elite / High / Medium / Low) are useful for context but the lever is the trend within your team.

rule.statement: Every P1/P2 incident from the quarter produces ONE learning extracted into the retro doc: the systemic cause + the systemic fix. Incident-level "what happened" goes in the postmortem; cross-incident pattern goes in the retro. If no learnings can be extracted, the postmortems weren't done properly.
rule.rationale: Quarter retros that ignore incidents repeat them. Each incident has a generalizable lesson if the postmortem found a real root cause. Surfacing 3-5 incident-learnings per quarter prevents the team from re-fighting the same outage shape.

rule.statement: Final retro doc is 4-6 pages. Longer = nobody reads it; shorter = it's a memo, not a retro. The format pressure forces themes to be sharp and Q+1 actions to be prioritized. Long-tail observations go to an appendix that's optional reading.

rationale.source: executive-doc length research; Amazon 6-page memo discipline

rule.rationale: Senior stakeholders skim docs > 6 pages and miss the asks. The 6-page cap is the canonical executive-readable format (cf. Amazon's 6-page memo). Themes that don't fit are either too narrow (single-squad) or insufficiently prioritized.

example.title: Multi-source theme with DORA + incident + squad retros
example.code:
Theme: Deploy pipeline friction Coverage: Squads A, B, D (3/5) Severity: 3 (high)
Evidence: [DORA delta] Lead time for changes: 4.2d → 6.8d (+62%) regression Q-over-Q [Squad A retro L34]: "Pipeline failures spiked in March, blocked deploys 2-3 days at a time" [Squad B retro L78]: "Flaky CI tests pushed deploy windows to weekends" [Squad D retro L19]: "Lost a sprint to investigating phantom test failures" [Incident INC-2026-014]: P2 root cause was a CI test that hides a race condition that also blocked deploys 8 times this quarter
Q+1 process change: Owner: SRE Lead Deadline: 2026-07-31 Action: Audit + harden the top-10 flaky tests; introduce quarantine policy; DORA lead-time target back to 4.5d or better. Success: Q3 lead time ≤ 5.0d AND CI failure rate < 5%.
example.note: Illustrates all 5 rules: 4-source coverage, multi-squad presence, DORA delta, incident learning, sharp Q+1.

example.title: Squad-narrative retro with no cross-cutting view
example.code:
"Squad A had a tough quarter. Squad B did well. Squad C struggled with hiring. Squad D shipped on time. We learned a lot."
Problem: violates r1 (no DORA, no incidents), r2 (no themes across squads), r3 (no trends), r4 (no incident learnings), r5 (it's a narrative summary, not a retro). Repair: ingest the 4 sources; cluster into multi-squad themes.
example.note: "What each squad did" is a status report, not a retro.
